behaviors.description: Renamed files appear only in the renamed category, not as added+deleted.
behaviors.justification: Feature map: wiring=yes, real_infra=yes, model_judgment=no, multi_step_agentic=no. By rule order, real_infra => System (and no higher-priority feature applies). This is best validated against real git rename semantics rather than mocked parsing alone.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI summary, and assert: renamed count increments, added/deleted do not include that file, and report category membership is exclusive for the rename.

behaviors.description: Binary-file diffs do not crash the tool and are counted in output.
behaviors.justification: Feature map: wiring=yes, real_infra=yes, model_judgment=no, multi_step_agentic=no. By rule order, real_infra => System. Binary diff handling can differ in real git output/exit behavior, so real repository execution is the minimum reliable level.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify binary files across commits, run the CLI, and assert process exits successfully and binary-changed files are reflected in category counts.

behaviors.description: A repository with no commits yields an empty report, not an error.
behaviors.justification: Feature map: wiring=yes, real_infra=yes, model_judgment=no, multi_step_agentic=no. By rule order, real_infra => System. The no-HEAD state is a real git edge case that often fails only when exercising actual repo commands.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with zero commits, run the CLI, and assert successful exit plus an empty structured report (zero counts/categories empty) instead of exception/error output.

behaviors.description: Reported counts match actual repository change history.
behaviors.justification: Feature map: wiring=yes, real_infra=yes, model_judgment=no, multi_step_agentic=no. By rule order, real_infra => System. Correctness here depends on end-to-end interaction with true git history traversal, not just internal counting logic.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a repo history containing known numbers of adds/deletes/renames/modifies over several commits, run the CLI, and assert each reported count equals the expected ground truth from that history.

behaviors.description: Output is identical for equivalent staged vs unstaged changes.
behaviors.justification: Feature map: wiring=yes, real_infra=yes, model_judgment=no, multi_step_agentic=no. By rule order, real_infra => System. Staging/index behavior is git-infrastructure-specific and requires real repo state transitions.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare equivalent change sets in two runs (one unstaged in working tree, one staged in index), invoke CLI for each, and assert the structured outputs are identical.
